A **lower house** is the lower [chamber](/wiki/Debate_chamber "Debate chamber") of a [bicameral](/wiki/Bicameralism "Bicameralism") [legislature](/wiki/Legislature "Legislature"), where the other chamber is the [upper house](/wiki/Upper_house "Upper house").[[1]](#cite_note-1) Although styled as "below" the upper house, in many legislatures worldwide, the lower house has come to wield more power or otherwise exert significant political influence.

## Common attributes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lower_house&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Common attributes")]

In comparison with the upper house, lower houses frequently display certain characteristics (though they vary by jurisdiction).

### Powers

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lower_house&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Powers")]

In a [parliamentary system](/wiki/Parliamentary_system "Parliamentary system"), the lower house:

* In the modern era, has much more power, usually due to restrictions on the upper house.
  + Exceptions to this are [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia"), [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy"), and [Romania](/wiki/Romania "Romania"), where the upper and lower houses have similar power.
* Is able to override the upper house in some ways.
* Can vote a [motion of no confidence](/wiki/Motion_of_no_confidence "Motion of no confidence") against the [government](/wiki/Government "Government"), as well as vote for or against any proposed candidate for head of government at the beginning of the parliamentary term.

In a [presidential system](/wiki/Presidential_system "Presidential system"), the lower house:

* Generally has less power than the upper house, but maintains exclusive powers in some areas.
* Has the sole power to impeach the executive; the upper house then tries the [impeachment](/wiki/Impeachment "Impeachment").
* Typically initiates legislation on appropriation and supply.

### Status

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lower_house&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Status")]

The lower house:

* Can, in a parliamentary system, be dissolved by the executive.
* Has total or initial control over budget, supply, and monetary laws.
* Has a lower [age of candidacy](/wiki/Age_of_candidacy "Age of candidacy") than the upper house.
* Is more numerous than the upper house.
  + The [Parliament of the United Kingdom](/wiki/Parliament_of_the_United_Kingdom "Parliament of the United Kingdom") furnishes a notable exception.

Members of the lower house:

* Are [elected directly](/wiki/Direct_election "Direct election"), while those of the upper house may hold their positions through direct or [indirect election](/wiki/Indirect_election "Indirect election"), appointment, or inheritance.
* Are elected more frequently, and all at once, not by staggered terms.
* Are usually numbered [in proportion to the population](/wiki/Proportional_representation "Proportional representation") of their [administrative divisions](/wiki/Administrative_division "Administrative division"), unlike in the upper house.

The government of the day is usually required to present [its budget](/wiki/Government_budget "Government budget") to the lower house, which must approve the budget. It is a widespread practice for revenue (appropriation) bills to originate in the lower house. A notable exception to this is the [West Virginia House of Delegates](/wiki/West_Virginia_House_of_Delegates "West Virginia House of Delegates") in the United States, which allows revenue bills to originate from either house.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Titles of lower houses

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lower_house&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Titles of lower houses")]

Many lower houses are named in manners such as follows:

* [Chamber of Deputies](/wiki/Chamber_of_Deputies "Chamber of Deputies")
* [Chamber of Representatives](/wiki/Chamber_of_Representatives_(disambiguation) "Chamber of Representatives (disambiguation)")
* [House of Assembly](/wiki/House_of_Assembly "House of Assembly")
* [House of Commons](/wiki/House_of_Commons "House of Commons")
* [House of Delegates](/wiki/House_of_Delegates_(disambiguation) "House of Delegates (disambiguation)")
* [House of the People](/wiki/House_of_the_People_(disambiguation) "House of the People (disambiguation)") (including [Lok Sabha](/wiki/Lok_Sabha "Lok Sabha"))
* [House of Representatives](/wiki/House_of_Representatives "House of Representatives")
* [Legislative Assembly](/wiki/Legislative_Assembly "Legislative Assembly")
* [National Assembly](/wiki/National_Assembly "National Assembly")
* [National Council](/wiki/National_Council_(disambiguation)#Legislative_bodies "National Council (disambiguation)")
* [Sejm](/wiki/Sejm "Sejm")/[Seimas](/wiki/Seimas "Seimas")
